skill.name: react-components
skill.description: Converts Stitch design screens into modular Vite and React components with reusable templates, style guidance, and local validation.
skill.summary: Generate modular React components from Stitch designs with validation and theme mapping.
skill.author: google-labs-code

security_audit.summary: AI review did not confirm malicious intent or prompt injection. Most high and medium static alerts are false positives from Markdown backticks, Apache license URLs, relative imports, and descriptive text, but the skill does intentionally run a Bash curl downloader and a filesystem-based validator.

medium_findings.title: External Network Fetch Through Bash Script
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run a Bash script that passes a Stitch download URL to curl and writes the response to a local output path. This is an intended workflow, but it allows external network retrieval from a URL supplied by Stitch metadata and should be published with a warning.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command path is explicit and the curl invocation is real. The risk is bounded because the purpose is design asset retrieval and no evidence of secret exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Broad Agent Tool Permissions
medium_findings.description: The skill declares access to Bash, Read, Write, web_fetch, and Stitch tools. These permissions match the design-to-code workflow, but they increase the effect of compromised design metadata or unsafe user prompts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The permissions and execution steps are directly visible in the skill file. There is no malicious instruction, but the capability set is materially broader than a documentation-only skill.

low_findings.title: Local Validator Reads Caller-Supplied Path
low_findings.description: The validation script reads the path passed on the command line and parses it with SWC. This is legitimate local validation, but callers should pass project component files rather than arbitrary sensitive paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read is direct and caller-controlled. The script only prints validation results and no network exfiltration path was found.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer False Positives Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak crypto, Ruby backtick execution, hardcoded URL, path traversal, and reconnaissance alerts mostly point to Markdown code fences, Apache license URLs, normal relative imports, package descriptions, and documentation text. AI review found no evidence of cryptographic misuse, Ruby execution, malware staging, or prompt injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual line review shows benign documentation, license, and TypeScript template content at the flagged locations. No suspicious override language or hidden execution was identified.

dangerous_patterns.title: curl Download to Local File
dangerous_patterns.description: The shell script accepts a URL and output path, then runs curl with redirects enabled and writes the response to disk. This pattern is useful for signed design downloads but should remain visible to users during installation review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The downloader is concrete executable code with network and filesystem effects. It appears functional rather than malicious because it uses explicit arguments and has no credential collection logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command-Driven Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill tells agents to install dependencies, run validation commands, and start a development server. These are normal development steps, but they require user awareness because they execute local commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command instructions are explicit and tied to the stated React workflow. No evidence shows obfuscated or destructive commands.

content.user_title: Convert Stitch Designs to React Components
content.value_statement: Design handoff often creates large React files that are hard to maintain. This skill turns Stitch screens into modular Vite and React components with theme mapping and validation steps.
actual_capabilities: Retrieves Stitch screen metadata through the configured Stitch MCP tools.
Downloads referenced Stitch HTML through a provided curl script.
Guides component splitting into data, hooks, and reusable React files.
Provides a TypeScript component template with props typing conventions.
Maps extracted Tailwind theme values to the local style guide.
Runs an SWC-based validator for props interfaces and hardcoded hex colors.
limitations: Requires access to a working Stitch MCP server and valid screen metadata.
Uses Bash and network access to download signed HTML assets.
Does not guarantee pixel-perfect output without manual visual review.
The validator checks limited rules and is not a full security scanner.

use_cases.title: Convert a Stitch Screen
use_cases.description: Generate a modular React component system from a selected Stitch screen and its downloaded HTML.

use_cases.title: Standardize Design Handoff
use_cases.description: Apply shared TypeScript, Tailwind, data, and hook conventions when moving designs into a Vite app.

use_cases.title: Review Generated Components
use_cases.description: Run a focused validator that checks props interfaces and hardcoded color usage before code review.

prompt_templates.title: Convert One Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner conversion from a known Stitch screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the selected Stitch screen into a modular React component system. Use the local template, create mock data, and run the validator.

prompt_templates.title: Map Theme Tokens
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract the Tailwind theme values from the Stitch HTML and align the generated components with resources/style-guide.json.

prompt_templates.title: Split Component Logic
prompt_templates.prompt: Refactor the generated screen into reusable components, a mock data file, and custom hooks for interaction logic.

prompt_templates.title: Validate and Repair Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the architecture checklist and validator on every generated component, then fix missing props interfaces and hardcoded styles.

output_examples.input: A Stitch activity feed screen needs to become a reusable React component set.
output: A component breakdown with container, card, avatar, badge, and metadata elements.
A mock data module that stores static text, image URLs, and list entries.
A validation summary describing missing props interfaces or hardcoded colors.

output_examples.input: A generated component uses raw hex values and mixed inline content.
output: A revised component plan that replaces raw colors with theme-mapped Tailwind classes.
A data extraction plan for moving labels, URLs, and repeated entries into mock data.
A concise checklist of remaining manual visual review items.

best_practices: Review the signed download URL before allowing the Bash fetch step.
Keep generated static text, image URLs, and list data in a separate mock data file.
Run validation and a browser visual check before accepting generated components.
anti_patterns: Do not run the fetch script with untrusted URLs from outside the expected Stitch workflow.
Do not place all generated UI, data, and logic into one large component file.
Do not treat the validator as a substitute for accessibility, security, or product review.

faq.question: Does this skill require network access?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses Stitch MCP metadata and a curl script to download signed HTML or screenshot assets.

faq.question: Can it create a full Vite React project?
faq.answer: It focuses on components inside a target project. Project setup may still need separate commands or manual changes.

faq.question: Does it support TypeScript?
faq.answer: Yes. The template and validation guidance expect TypeScript React components with props interfaces.

faq.question: Will the output be pixel perfect?
faq.answer: No. It improves design fidelity, but final layout and responsive behavior need human review.

faq.question: What does the validator check?
faq.answer: It checks for a props interface ending in Props and className values with hardcoded hex colors.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It can be published with a warning. Users should understand that it runs local commands and performs external downloads.
